claim: Claude Code is a pure agent architecture using simple instructions, powerful tools (file creation/editing, terminal), and a loop design—not RAG-based indexing—to explore codebases the way a human would
actionable: Understand that Claude Code explores your codebase using agentic search (glob, grep, find) rather than embeddings; this means it discovers context dynamically like a developer onboarding to a team
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Claude Code.md files in project or home directories persist knowledge and instructions across sessions without using memory—this is the primary mechanism for sharing context with teams and across sessions
actionable: Create and maintain claude.md files in your project root with style guides, project architecture, testing procedures, and key patterns; keep them in version control for team alignment
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Claude Code is multimodal and can take screenshots or images as input; you can paste a design mockup and ask it to build the website or UI based on the visual
actionable: When building UIs from designs, grab a screenshot of the mockup and paste it directly into Claude Code with a request to implement the design
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Using '/clear' resets context except for claude.md; using '/compact' summarizes conversation for handoff to another developer or session continuation without full context
actionable: When approaching context limits (200k tokens), use '/compact' to create a summary for the next session; this lets you continue work across multiple sessions efficiently
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Planning and asking Claude Code for options before implementing (without writing files yet) saves time by validating approach before committing to code
actionable: Start by asking Claude Code to search the codebase and propose 2-3 implementation approaches; validate your preferred option before proceeding to actual implementation
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: You can run multiple Claude Code instances in parallel (4+ instances) by using tmux, different tabs, or other multiplexing tools for orchestrated multi-agent workflows
actionable: Set up multiple Claude Code instances in parallel tmux panes or separate tabs to work on different features simultaneously while maintaining separate contexts
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Pressing 'escape' pauses Claude Code and lets you interject; pressing 'escape' twice lets you jump back in your conversation history and reset tool expansion
actionable: Use escape to stop Claude Code if you notice it going off track; double-escape to revisit earlier conversation points and reset the reasoning state
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Claude 4 models now support thinking between tool calls (not just in the initial prompt), which is critical for complex problem-solving during codebase exploration and bug diagnosis
actionable: Use 'think hard' or extended thinking directives in Claude Code when working on complex bugs or architectural decisions to let the model reason between search operations
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Installing CLI tools and MCP servers (like the GitHub gh CLI or Sonar MCP) gives Claude Code more powerful tools to work with; well-documented CLI tools are generally better than MCP for core functionality
actionable: Install popular CLI tools (git, Docker, gh, etc.) on your system for Claude Code to use; use MCP servers for specialized integrations not covered by standard CLIs
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Using test-driven development with Claude Code (write tests, make small changes, verify tests pass) and committing regularly reduces the risk of going off the rails
actionable: Ask Claude Code to write tests first; have it make small focused changes; verify tests pass after each change; commit regularly so you can fall back if needed
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude

claim: Claude Code can be used headlessly via the SDK for programmatic integration in CI/CD pipelines, GitHub Actions, and automated workflows—not just interactive development
actionable: Explore headless Claude Code usage in your GitHub Actions and CI/CD pipelines for automated code generation, testing, and deployment tasks
source_title: Claude Code best practices | Code w/ Claude
